channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Yelp has quietly become a home services powerhouse
item.description: When the COVID-19 pandemic abruptly shut down normal life more than four years ago, few tech companies endured more wrenching change than Yelp.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Beware of fraudsters posing as government officials trying to steal your cash
item.description: CISA warns of scammers impersonating its officials. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges people to be alert and verify contacts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Swiss glaciers are receding again after 2 punishing years and despite a good start to 2024
item.description: Experts say the volume of Switzerland’s glaciers shrank again this summer and compounded the negative impact of climate change after a devastating two-year run that depleted the ice by more than 10%.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge partially dismisses FTC's antitrust lawsuit against Amazon
item.description: A federal judge on Monday partially dismissed the Federal Trade Commission’s (FTC) antitrust lawsuit against Amazon. However, few details are currently available about U.S. District Judge John Chun’s decision, which was filed under seal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Russian national indicted in ransomware attacks in US
item.description: A Russian national is accused in a series of ransomware attacks in Texas and other parts of the U.S. over a seven-year period, the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: All the major legal battles Elon Musk and his companies are facing
item.description: Elon Musk and his companies are embroiled in dozens of lawsuits, ranging from fatal crashes blamed on Tesla Inc.’s Autopilot system to civil rights complaints over the treatment of employees.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Scammers’ sneaky new tactic preying on people who’ve lost their iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to protect yourself with eight steps if your iPhone is lost or stolen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US modifies AI chip export rules, boosting Middle East access
item.description: In a move aimed at facilitating the global development and supply of artificial intelligence (AI), the US Commerce Department has brought a new policy that will ease export restrictions for advanced AI chips to data centers in the Middle East and Central Asia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to use Loop components in Microsoft 365 apps
item.description: Microsoft’s ambitious collaboration app, Microsoft Loop, includes shared workspaces as well as portable content snippets called Loop components. These components can be shared and embedded in multiple Microsoft 365 apps.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Inside the trials and tribulations of a major ransomware attack
item.description: Like many small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), ‘JPX Construction’ didn’t see itself as a potential target for a ransomware attack. It wasn’t a high-profile organisation. It didn’t seem to hold much high-value data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The worst programmer I know
item.description: If all code is legacy code, then we are all maintainers. I carry an image in my head of a vast sea of silent, diligent, dedicated coders who silently maintain much of the code that makes the world go round. I carry this image because I am one of those coders.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JRuby 10 due to arrive in early-2025
item.description: JRuby, which dates back to 2001 as a Ruby language implementation for the JVM, is set to arrive at version 10 shortly after the new year. JRuby 10 promises to be fully compatible with Ruby 3.4.0, a planned update to Ruby that brings changes for frozen string literals and class updates.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: The decision is a blow to efforts attempting to rein in the industry that is rapidly evolving with little oversight.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Examining the many ways to run loops on Linux
item.description: The bash shell provides numerous ways to loop through data to make your work – especially scripting challenges – easier. You can loop through a pile of data so you can get a lot done just by running a single script, and you can generate and loop through different sequences of values.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Zero Trust + AI: A match made in the clouds
item.description: Have any terms in the cybersecurity space generated more buzz than “zero trust” and “AI”? At the risk of sounding like a Magic 8 Ball, my reply is no.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Demystifying AI for the public sector
item.description: Government agencies are understandably hesitant about artificial intelligence. But with the right partners, AI can revolutionize government services. The post Demystifying AI for the public sector first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Kristen Bell is a Meta AI voice after criticizing company in post
item.description: After posting an "opt out" declaration on Instagram, the actress has now signed with Meta to use her voice in its AI chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.), chairwoman of the Oversight and Accountability Subcommittee on Cybersecurity, IT and Innovation, is asking for a briefing by Oct. 3. The post Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Ukraine shows off capabilities of new drone-dropped, flamethrowing robotic war dogs: video
item.description: The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense shared a video of a robotic war dog being used in the field with a soldier, who was able to take advantage of the device's thermal imaging.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: NTSB engineer says carbon fiber hull from submersible showed signs of flaws
item.description: The carbon fiber hull of the experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreckage of the Titanic had imperfections dating to the manufacturing process and behaved differently after a loud bang was heard on one of the dives the year before the tragedy, an engineer with the National Transpo
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: House cyber workforce bill pushes two-year degrees for gov service
item.description: House Homeland Security Committee Chairman Mark Green's cyber workforce bill adds to a growing push to embrace skills-based hiring and training for cyber jobs. The post House cyber workforce bill pushes two-year degrees for gov service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: DARPA wraps up semifinal of artificial intelligence challenge
item.description: "Inside that synthetic version of the code, we inserted vulnerabilities for the competitors to find," AI Cyber Challenge program manager Andrew Carney said. The post DARPA wraps up semifinal of artificial intelligence challenge first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: X releases its first transparency report since Elon Musk's takeover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Social media platform X on Wednesday published its first transparency report since the company was purchased by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4
